game_name: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

danger_signals.visual_form: Kremling reptilians, porcupines, bees, vultures (land); pufferfish, stingrays, starfish, piranhas (water)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or combat via jump/cartwheel/barrel throw

danger_signals.visual_form: Fog, rain, thunderstorms in specific levels; spike-laden surfaces; breakable walls concealing bonus traps
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced visibility or damage-causing terrain requiring careful navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Red and yellow Zinger palettes (bee-like flying enemies)
danger_signals.meaning: Mid-air threat in specific zones; aerial navigation hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: King K. Rool at world culminations; Kaptain K. Rool in Krocodile Kore (final fight)
danger_signals.meaning: World progression gate; mandatory defeat to advance; K. Rool fights have unused death music indicating threat severity

danger_signals.visual_form: Active character runs away when hit; control switches to partner; both dying = life lost
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; loss of one character narrows tactical options

safety_signals.meaning: Safe retrieval point for separated partner; restores team capability
Safe respawn point within level; loss reduces restart distance
Temporary immunity state; brief safe passage through enemy clusters

safety_signals.visual_form: Barrels containing Squitter, Rambi, Rattly, Enguarde, Squawks
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary ally acquisition; enhanced movement/attack capability
Extra life acquisition; life pool expansion = safety buffer

safety_signals.visual_form: Four letters spelling KONG scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Extra life reward upon completion; safety resource accumulation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: DK Barrel marking; Kong Kollege signage; Monkey Museum
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diddy Kong (agile, faster) vs Dixie Kong (higher jump, hair-spin glide) mechanical differentiation signals cooperative family structure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kong family member locations: Cranky Kong at Monkey Museum, Wrinkly Kong at Kong Kollege, Swanky Kong at quiz show venue, Funky Kong at aircraft hub

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crocodile Isle setting; King K. Rool leadership; Kaptain K. Rool helm variant in final boss
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kremling reptilians (land-based); Flotsam (blue/green water variants, unused red/yellow palettes); enemy palette shifts indicate ecological adaptation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crocodile Isle geography; eight worlds totaling 52 levels under Kremling occupation; Klubba's Kiosk as Kremling trade post (demands Kremkoins for passage)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Donkey Kong kidnapped by King K. Rool
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game title/narrative setup; DK absence from playable roster; Diddy/Dixie as replacement protagonists
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate 8 worlds instead of familiar single-character progression; partnership mechanic becomes mandatory (character switching on damage)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight distinct world environments (Kongo Jungle variant, pirate ship themes, castle levels, industrial zones); each world contains 4-8 levels totaling environmental saturation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progressive world-by-world boss gauntlet; each boss defeat = territorial reclamation signal; world completion unlocks next island sector

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skull-shaped roller coaster; fortress/castle level designs; industrial/mechanical trap setups; barrel-cannon systems (both helpful and hostile)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level design emphasizes navigation through constructed hazards; environmental interaction (jumping pads with reward targets) signals engineered rather than natural threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bonus world accessible after collecting sufficient Kremkoins; unlocks access to true ending and Kaptain K. Rool rematch
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Additional progression path beyond 8 main worlds; 102% completion requires Lost World collection; K. Rool Duel and Krocodile Kore feature unused K. Rool victory/death music indicating narrative weight

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Breakable walls (right-side castle wall sprite unused, left-facing variants visible); hidden barrel entrances; cannon-activated challenge zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional challenge levels reward Kremcoin collection; Kremcoin accumulation = Lost World access gate; bonus completion = completionist world-state tracking

environmental_storytelling.example: Castle levels (Castle Crush, Chain Link Chamber, Toxic Tower, Stronghold Showdown) with concealed bonus entrances communicate Kremling stronghold construction; design redundancy (only 2 of 4 have visible left-facing breakable wall entrances) suggests hastily-built or repurposed fortifications

Water-level enemies (pufferfish, stingrays, starfish, piranhas) with distinct sprite palettes (Flotsam red/yellow/blue/green, Flitter turquoise/purple/red variants) suggest Kremling control over diverse ecological zones; unused palettes indicate abandoned environmental designs

Playable animals (Squitter spider, Rambi rhino, Rattly snake, Enguarde swordfish, Squawks parrot) positioned in levels indicate Kong alliance network; each animal unlocks specific terrain (Rambi charges through barriers, Squawks flies, Rattly reaches heights) = environmental access maps Kong resource distribution

Klubba's Kiosk as Kremling checkpoint demanding 15 Kremkoins per world access communicates economic control; Kremcoin collection rate gates progression, indicating resource scarcity in occupied territories

Named family member locations (Cranky at Monkey Museum providing secrets/comic relief, Wrinkly at Kong Kollege offering educational guidance, Swanky at quiz show, Funky at aircraft hub) communicate safe-zone network and resistance infrastructure within Kremling-occupied space

Fog, rain, thunderstorms in specific levels reduce visibility = danger amplification without additional enemies; environmental effects communicate natural world responding to occupation or seasonal/temporal narrative progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Higher run speed, lower jump height; enables faster horizontal traversal but limited vertical reach; cartwheel attack indicates offensive capability
Lower run speed, higher jump height, hair-spin glide mechanic; enables vertical navigation and aerial sustained movement; first appearance suggests protagonist role expansion
Character runs away offscreen; control shifts to partner automatically; signals temporary incapacity and tactical reorganization requirement

Land-based varieties (reptilians, porcupines, bees, vultures) position in level layouts; defeat via jump/cartwheel/barrel indicates vulnerability to player tactics; palette variants (unused green Zinger, red/yellow Flotsam, multiple Flitter colors) suggest unit-type differentiation not fully utilized in final build
Pufferfish, stingrays, starfish, piranhas confined to underwater sections; mechanical separation from land threats indicates environmental specialization; restrict player breathing/timing windows

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss entities (King K. Rool / Kaptain K. Rool)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mandatory defeat requirement for world progression; unused music assets (Death against K. Rool, Diddy wins, Dixie wins, K. Rool Duel themes) indicate narrative branching in design phase; visual transformation (King to Kaptain variant) signals escalating threat

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Animal friend (Rambi, Squitter, Rattly, Enguarde, Squawks)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Unique movement/attack abilities activate upon mount; Rambi charges barriers, Squawks flies and spits eggs, Rattly jumps extremely high, Enguarde sword-breaks obstacles, Squitter web-swings; ability set determines environment accessibility = spatial gating through ally mechanics
Static positioning marks intermediate restart point; loss of checkpoint means longer replay distance; visual stability communicates safe node within hostile level

notes: DKC2 uses spatial and color-based faction differentiation with minimal explicit UI narrative. Kremling identity emerges through architectural design (castles, industrial traps), enemy palette variety, and trade-post mechanics (Klubba's Kiosk). Kong faction communicated through family member safe-zones and animal ally integration rather than faction colors/symbols. Environmental storytelling prioritizes ecological stratification (water vs land enemies) and infrastructure control (Crocodile Isle occupation mapped through eight world sectors). Unused asset evidence (green Zinger, red/yellow Flotsam, alternate Flitter palettes, sad Kong sprites, unused warp rooms, German language option) indicates design iterations emphasizing enemy/NPC visual differentiation beyond final implementation. K. Rool dual-form (King to Kaptain) and unused boss music suggest narrative complexity in protagonist vs antagonist confrontation originally planned. 102% completion mechanic and Lost World unlock create meta-world-state progression beyond main 8 worlds, indicating hidden resistance territory or post-victory challenge realm.
